meaning: To make something unclear or difficult to understand.
simple_explanation: When someone purposely confuses you with complicated words or ideas to hide the truth.
example: The politician tried to obfuscate the real reason for the policy change with technical jargon.
mnemonic: Think of a magician creating a cloud of smoke (fus = fuse/fog) to hide what's really happening.

meaning: Lasting for a very short time; transitory.
simple_explanation: Something that comes and goes quickly, like a soap bubble that pops almost immediately.
example: The morning frost was ephemeral, disappearing as soon as the sun rose.
mnemonic: Think 'a few hours' ‚Äî ephemeral things don't last long at all.

meaning: Persistent and determined; not giving up easily.
simple_explanation: Like a dog that won't let go of a toy ‚Äî someone tenacious just keeps going no matter what.
example: Despite numerous setbacks, her tenacious pursuit of excellence eventually led to success.
mnemonic: TEN-acious: imagine someone holding on for 10 more rounds when everyone else has quit.

meaning: Tending to talk a great deal; overly talkative.
simple_explanation: That one friend who just can't stop talking ‚Äî loquacious people love to talk and talk.
example: Our loquacious tour guide kept the group entertained with endless stories throughout the trip.
mnemonic: LOQUA sounds like 'talk-wa' ‚Äî a loquacious person is always talking.

meaning: Having a ready insight; shrewd and having a clear understanding.
simple_explanation: Someone who is perspicacious can see through complicated situations quickly, like a detective who spots clues others miss.
example: The perspicacious investor identified the startup's potential long before it became profitable.
mnemonic: Per-SPEC-acious: think of someone with perfect SPECS (glasses) who sees everything clearly.
